title: Fanout Exchanges and True Publish/Subscribe · Type-System Trace: Ephemeral Subscription
summary: Audit, notification, search, and analytics consumers share one queue and steal work from each other; a fanout exchange exists but late or disconnected subscriber behavior is undocumented. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ephemeral subscription increment in fanout subscription topology and lifecycle map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design exclusive server-named subscriptions for session-scoped state and account for disconnect, binding, declaration, and cleanup races.
Use fanout routing when every binding should match and record the resulting copy count and storage responsibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Ephemeral Subscription · Theory Ephemeral Subscription
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 1042 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove one subscription queue per responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; keep every queue and subscriber responsibility explicitly named; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Ephemeral Subscription contract for the supplied data.
